vitals.location: Mangshi to Jinghong, Yunnan, China (multi-city stage race)
vitals.date: 2026: early November (13th edition, exact dates not yet published)
route_options: Stage 1: 116 km (Mangshi area, includes Jiandong Mountain climb, ~1,000 m gain)
Stage 2: 160 km (2025: shortened by heavy rain to 137 km)
Stage 3: 158 km (2025: shortened and neutralized after landslides/road collapse)
Stage 5: 133 km, ~2,500 m climbing, finishing Jinghong/Xishuangbanna area

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 69. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 64. FLAG (2026-07-24 editorial wave 7): this is a 5-stage, ~650+km multi-day stage race (organizer's own format), not a single 140km/2,286m one-day fondo — the prior vitals appear to be an unsourced single-stage extract. distance=3 and every other dimension were scored against the wrong event shape entirely; recommend a human re-score against the actual multi-stage format, and consider whether discipline should be multi_stage rather than gran_fondo (see catalog_flags). Numbers left untouched per no-silent-reshuffle rule.

course_description.character: A multi-city, multi-day timed stage race across western Yunnan, not a single one-day fondo. The 2025 (12th) edition ran five GC stages totaling roughly 650+ km plus a non-GC 9 km Duo TT prologue, moving through Mangshi, Tengchong, Fengqing, Pu'er/Simao, and finishing in the Xishuangbanna/Jinghong area — Kunming was not the 2025 event base, despite this database's prior listing. General classification is genuinely competitive (active UCI professionals are excluded), with traffic-controlled stages and organizer-run inter-city bus/hotel logistics between stage towns.
course_description.signature_challenge: Stage 1's Jiandong Mountain climb (~1,000 m gain) sets the GC tone early; Stage 5 (133 km, ~2,500 m climbing) into the Jinghong/Xishuangbanna finish closes out the tour as the hardest single day.
suffering_zones: Jiandong Mountain (Stage 1) — the tour's first major selection point, ~1,000 m of gain
Stage 5 (133 km / ~2,500 m) — the hardest closing day, finishing in the Xishuangbanna/Jinghong area

Weather and terrain disruption is a real, documented risk, not a hypothetical: heavy rain shortened Stage 2 to 137 km in 2025, and landslides/a road collapse forced Stage 3 to be shortened and neutralized that same year

logistics.transport: Organizer-run inter-city bus transfers and hotel packages between stage towns; multiple long transfers scheduled across the tour.

history.origin_story: Founded in November 2014 as a multi-stage amateur road race across western Yunnan. Continuity has been uneven: reduced to a single stage in 2020, cancelled entirely in 2021, returned with a four-day format in 2022 and 2023, and restored to five stages in 2024. The organizer's own edition numbering (2023/2024/2025/2026 labeled 10th/11th/12th/13th) appears to count a cancelled or otherwise unlisted edition — treat the numbering as organizer branding, not proof that twelve full stage races had actually been ridden by 2025.
notable_moments: 2024-2025: Five-stage format restored, 2025 edition run October 31-November 8 with 2,000+ participants

biased_opinion.verdict: A Real Stage Race, Mislabeled as a One-Day Fondo
biased_opinion.summary: Granfondo Yunnan is a genuine, competitive multi-day amateur stage race — five GC stages across roughly 650+ km through western Yunnan's Mangshi, Tengchong, Fengqing, Pu'er and Xishuangbanna regions, plus a non-GC Duo TT prologue. That's a materially bigger and more complicated undertaking than the single 140 km/2,286m Kunming fondo this database previously described, which appears to have been an unsourced single-stage extract rather than the actual event. The 2025 edition drew more than 2,000 riders and delivered real GC racing with professional exclusions — but it also delivered real disruption, with heavy rain shortening one stage and landslides forcing another to be neutralized. This is a rare amateur stage-race experience, not a weekend fondo, and should be marketed and scored as such.
strengths: Genuinely rare format for amateur cyclists: a real multi-day GC stage race with timed classification, not a single-day mass ride
Substantial field (2,000+ in 2025) and organizer-run inter-city logistics (buses, hotel packages) that lower the coordination burden of a multi-city tour
Real geographic and cultural variety across western Yunnan's Mangshi-Tengchong-Fengqing-Xishuangbanna corridor
Traffic-controlled, competitive racing with professional-rider exclusions to keep the amateur GC meaningful
weaknesses: Considerably more complicated and expensive than a one-day fondo — multiple stage towns, long transfers, and multi-day logistics
Documented weather and terrain risk: 2025 saw one stage shortened by heavy rain and another neutralized after landslides and a road collapse
2026 specifics (exact dates, cities, fees) are not yet published as of this review, and the organizer's own registration page still points to stale prior-year material
The organizer's edition-numbering (10th-13th) glosses over a cancelled 2021 edition and a reduced 2020 edition — don't take 'Nth edition' at face value
biased_opinion.bottom_line: If you want a genuine, competitive amateur stage race through dramatic western Yunnan terrain, this delivers — but budget for the logistics of a multi-city tour, not a weekend fondo, and go in with eyes open about weather-driven route changes.

final_verdict.one_liner: A ~650km, 5-stage amateur GC race across western Yunnan — not the single-day Kunming fondo this profile used to describe.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you want a rare, genuinely competitive multi-day amateur stage race with real GC stakes and don't mind the added logistics of a multi-city tour. Skip if you're looking for a simple one-day fondo — that's not what this is.
final_verdict.alternatives: For a comparable multi-day amateur stage race format, look at the Tour of Southland (New Zealand) or an established European amateur stage race; for a single-day Yunnan-region ride, look for a dedicated Kunming-area sportive instead.

catalog_flags.status_note: Taxonomy note: organizer markets this as 'Granfondo Yunnan' but the actual format (5 GC stages, ~650+km, multi-city tour with inter-city transfers) aligns much more closely with Roadie Labs' multi_stage discipline than gran_fondo. Not changing the discipline enum per editorial-wave scope — flagging for a human taxonomy decision.
